why_them: Built on WordPress with a custom BDSDigital theme, the site is functional but lacks a blog, FAQ section, and social media integration beyond a basic Facebook link. Despite trading since 1862 as a 6th-generation family business, there are no staff profiles and no visible Google reviews or ratings. No live chat or online enquiry form visible, which is a missed conversion opportunity for bereaved families searching out of hours.
recommended_service: SEO content strategy and blog build-out to capture long-tail funeral search terms in Dumfries
outreach_hook: Jardine's 160-year heritage deserves a website that actually ranks — yours has no blog or FAQ content capturing local search traffic.

why_them: The site runs on Wix Thunderbolt — a template-builder that produces generic output and limits SEO customisation. No phone number or email is visible in the header or footer, meaning bereaved families have to hunt for contact details at the worst possible moment. No testimonials, no blog, and no trust-building content are present despite the business holding SAIF membership since 1991.
recommended_service: Custom WordPress website with clear contact hierarchy, SEO optimisation, and trust-signal pages
outreach_hook: Your phone number isn't visible on the homepage — families in crisis clicking your Wix site can't find how to reach you.

why_them: Built on WordPress with the Astra theme and Elementor, the site is relatively modern and mobile-friendly. However, no email address is publicly displayed anywhere on the site, and Google reviews are referenced but not embedded. The business has traded since 2020 and has no blog or grief-support content to attract organic search traffic. Static content with no news or updates will depress search rankings over time.
recommended_service: SEO content programme and Google Reviews integration to build credibility for this newer entrant
outreach_hook: There's no email address anywhere on your site — potential clients who don't want to call have no way to reach you.

why_them: The site is the strongest of the group — custom WordPress theme, schema markup, customer testimonials, and social media integration are all present. Established in 1948 and run by the fourth generation of the McNeil family. The main gap is the absence of a blog or content hub, meaning the site is not capturing search traffic for funeral planning guides or pre-paid funeral terms in the Wigtownshire area.
recommended_service: Content marketing and local SEO to capture pre-need funeral searches across Wigtownshire
outreach_hook: Four generations of McNeil history and no blog means Google has nothing to rank you for when families search for funeral guidance in Stranraer.

why_them: Running on WordPress with GeneratePress, the site has a dated blue-and-serif aesthetic that looks roughly 8-10 years old. The Hotmail email address in public listings signals the business has not invested in professional digital infrastructure. No testimonials, no social media presence, and no blog despite 60 years of family trading history. The contact page lacks a lead form and relies entirely on a phone call.
recommended_service: Full website redesign with professional email setup, contact forms, and trust-building content
outreach_hook: A Hotmail contact address and 10-year-old design undermine the 60 years of trust the Bendall family has built in Castle Douglas.

why_them: Built on Wix, the site is a minimal template with no email address publicly displayed and very limited content depth. No testimonials, no staff profiles, no blog, and no pricing guidance. The business has branches in both Lockerbie and Lochmaben yet the site makes no effort to leverage local SEO for either town. Wix's inherent limitations around structured data and crawlability will suppress rankings for high-intent funeral searches.
recommended_service: Custom WordPress website with multi-location SEO, email capture, and transparent service pages
outreach_hook: Wix won't rank you for funeral searches in Lockerbie or Lochmaben — and with no email visible, you're losing enquiries before they start.

why_them: Built on Joomla with SP Page Builder, the site has a design aesthetic dating to around 2017 and looks noticeably dated. Despite holding NAFD accreditation, there is no testimonials section, no blog, and no FAQ content. The site serves both Annan and Longtown but does not have location-specific landing pages to capture searches from either community. Social media integration is absent.
recommended_service: Website modernisation and local SEO with Annan and Dumfriesshire location pages
outreach_hook: Your Joomla site hasn't had a design update since 2017 — it looks dated the moment a bereaved family lands on it.

why_them: No website exists at all — the business appears only on third-party funeral directory listings with no independent web presence. No email address found on any public source. As the only funeral director serving the Thornhill and Nithsdale area, the absence of a website means every bereaved family searching online cannot find them directly. A single well-built site would immediately dominate local search with no competition.
recommended_service: New website build with local SEO targeting Thornhill, Penpont, and Nithsdale funeral searches
outreach_hook: Families searching for a funeral director in Thornhill right now can't find you online — you have no website at all.
